metadata.summary: High-performance build system for JS/TS monorepos with intelligent caching and parallel execution. Every cacheable task must declare explicit outputs and dependsOn.

content.title: Monorepo Setup with Turborepo
content.summary: Turborepo is a high-performance build system for JavaScript/TypeScript monorepos. It provides intelligent caching, parallel task execution, and incremental builds across multiple packages and apps. The core rule: every cacheable task must declare explicit outputs and dependsOn; omitting either causes stale or no-cache builds.

why.p: Monorepos with shared packages waste CI time rebuilding unchanged code. Turborepo's content-hashed task cache eliminates redundant rebuilds — a 5-minute CI step becomes 5 seconds after the first run. Task pipeline declaration (dependsOn: ["^build"]) ensures correct execution order across workspace packages.

list.item: 2+ JS/TS apps sharing UI components, types, or utility packages
Solo or small team running web + admin + marketing site from one repo with shared lint/format/CI
Projects where CI build time is dominated by re-running unchanged work
Microservices in TS/JS where shared types (zod schemas, OpenAPI clients) need a single source of truth
Single app, no shared code — Turbo overhead exceeds benefit
Polyglot monorepos (Go + TS + Python) — Turbo only orchestrates JS scripts; use Bazel, Pants, or Moon instead
Projects needing sophisticated dependency graphs (codegen → build → release) — Nx or Bazel scales further
Teams already on Lerna or Yarn Workspaces with no perf pain — migration cost may not pay back

section.name: workspace-layout
section.p: Turborepo workspace structure with apps/, packages/, and root configuration.

rule.statement: Separate apps (runnable services) from packages (shared libraries) at the workspace root.
rule.rationale: Apps are app-shaped (no exports field); packages are library-shaped (with exports). Mixing them confuses Turborepo's dependency graph and publishing workflows.

example.title: Monorepo directory structure
example.code: monorepo/ ├── turbo.json ├── package.json # root: scripts delegate to turbo ├── pnpm-workspace.yaml ├── apps/ │ ├── web/ # Next.js app — no exports, private: true │ └── api/ # Express/Fastify service — no exports └── packages/ ├── ui/ # shared React components — has exports map ├── config/ # eslint, tsconfig presets — versionless ├── types/ # shared TS types — has exports map └── utils/ # shared utilities — has exports map

section.name: package-manager
section.p: Package manager selection and workspace protocol configuration.

rule.statement: Use pnpm with a pinned version in the root package.json packageManager field and add an engines.pnpm constraint.
rule.rationale: pnpm has the best workspace protocol support (workspace:*). Pinning prevents version drift across machines and CI. npm workspace hoisting causes phantom dependency bugs.

section.name: shared-package-conventions
section.p: Rules for managing shared packages within the workspace.

rule.statement: Keep config packages (eslint preset, tsconfig) at version 0.0.0 with private: true. Never publish them.
rule.rationale: Config packages are build-time tools consumed via workspace:*. Versioning them creates unnecessary churn; publishing them leaks internal configs.

rule.statement: Use workspace:* for internal dependencies in pnpm. Never use relative paths (../../packages/ui/src) — always @org/ui.
rule.rationale: Relative imports cross app/package boundaries and break when packages are moved or published. @org/ui via workspace:* resolves correctly in all contexts.

section.reference: Shared TypeScript base config for library and app packages

section.name: core-pipeline-rules
section.p: Essential turbo.json configuration for correct caching and task execution.

rule.statement: Every cacheable task must declare explicit outputs. Without outputs, Turborepo caches nothing useful and returns empty directories on cache hit.
rule.rationale: Turbo stores the file set listed in outputs as the cache artifact. An empty outputs: [] stores nothing; subsequent runs fetch an empty result and rebuild silently.

rule.statement: Declare every environment variable that affects build output in env (per-task) or globalEnv (all tasks). Undeclared env vars cause stale cache hits across configurations.
rule.rationale: If NODE_ENV or API_URL changes the build output but is not listed, Turbo returns the cached prod artifact for a dev build — a silent correctness bug.

rule.statement: Mark dev and start tasks with "cache": false, "persistent": true. Never cache long-running processes.
rule.rationale: Dev servers are non-deterministic and never complete. Caching them stores incomplete state; persistent: true tells Turbo to keep the process alive indefinitely.

section.name: dependson-patterns
section.p: Task dependency declaration patterns for correct build order.

rule.statement: Use "dependsOn": ["^build"] (caret prefix) for tasks that need upstream packages built first. Use "dependsOn": ["build"] (no caret) for tasks that need the same package built first.
rule.rationale: The caret ^ means "all dependencies of this package must complete this task first." Without caret, typecheck might run before the upstream package emits its type declarations, causing false errors.

example.title: Correct Turbo task pipeline
example.note: Turbo v2+ uses tasks key; v1 used pipeline. Always check the installed version before copy-pasting examples.

example.title: Missing dependsOn causes false errors
example.note: Missing dependsOn: typecheck may run before upstream packages emit declarations, producing false type errors.

section.name: cache-configuration
section.p: Output paths and environment variable management for cache correctness.

rule.statement: Ensure output paths in turbo.json match exactly what the build tool emits. Use --summarize to inspect what was actually cached.
rule.rationale: Turborepo caches an empty directory if outputs mismatch. Future CI runs get a cache hit with no files, then fail during the next task that consumes the outputs.

rule.statement: Set up remote cache from day one and enable signature verification ("signature": true) in shared environments.
rule.rationale: Without remote cache, every CI machine rebuilds from scratch. Without signature verification, a corrupt artifact uploaded by one machine breaks every consumer until manually purged.

section.reference: Reference turbo.json with correct pipeline, cache flags, and env declarations

section.name: structural-antipatterns
section.p: Common structural mistakes in monorepo setup.

antipattern.description: Circular package dependencies: packages/a depends on packages/b, which depends on packages/a.
antipattern.reason: Turborepo cannot compute a valid execution order for a cycle; the build hangs or fails with an opaque error. Extract the shared code into a third package with no internal dependencies.

antipattern.description: Phantom dependencies: package uses a library that works in development because it is hoisted to the root node_modules, but the package.json does not declare it as a dependency.
antipattern.reason: The build works locally but fails on a clean install or after publishing because the transitive dep is no longer guaranteed to be present. Always declare every import as an explicit dependency.

antipattern.description: Hardcoded relative imports across packages: import { Button } from '../../../packages/ui/src/button'.
antipattern.reason: Relative cross-package imports bypass the exports map, break on publish, and are invisible to Turborepo's dependency graph. Always use the package name: import { Button } from '@org/ui'.

section.name: cache-antipatterns
section.p: Common caching mistakes that cause stale builds and silent correctness bugs.

antipattern.description: Outputs path mismatch: turbo.json declares outputs: ["dist/**"] but the build tool emits to out/.
antipattern.reason: Turborepo caches an empty directory. Future CI runs get a cache hit with no files, then fail during the next task that consumes the outputs. Use --summarize to inspect what was actually cached.

antipattern.description: Undeclared environment variables in env / globalEnv that affect the build output.
antipattern.reason: The same task hash is computed for both NODE_ENV=development and NODE_ENV=production builds, so the prod artifact gets served as the dev build (or vice versa). Every env var that changes output must be declared.

antipattern.description: Caching dev and start tasks, or caching non-deterministic tasks.
antipattern.reason: Dev servers are non-deterministic and never complete. Caching them stores incomplete state and breaks subsequent runs that depend on the server being available.

antipattern.description: Caching tasks as dev when they should not be cached because they have side effects or random output.
antipattern.reason: Any task with "cache": false must also have "persistent": true to prevent Turborepo from treating the task as one-shot.

section.name: limitations-and-gotchas
section.p: Known constraints and version-specific issues with Turborepo.

rule.statement: Be aware that cache key bugs (missing inputs or wrong outputs in turbo.json) cause stale or no-cache builds; debugging requires --summarize and reading hashes.
rule.rationale: Cache correctness is invisible until a build fails mysteriously; always use --summarize to verify caching behavior.

rule.statement: Be aware that remote cache poisoning occurs when a corrupt artifact uploaded by one machine breaks every other consumer until manually purged.
rule.rationale: Enable signature verification in production environments to prevent this.

rule.statement: GlobalDependencies misconfig means env vars not declared cause silent cache hits across configs (e.g., prod build cached as dev).
rule.rationale: Every env var affecting output must be explicitly listed.

rule.statement: Be aware that task pipeline drift occurs when agents add dev tasks that get cached because they forgot "cache": false.
rule.rationale: Audit turbo.json after any agent modifications.

rule.statement: Workspace protocol gotchas: workspace:* works in pnpm/yarn but not npm <7; agents may pick the wrong package manager.
rule.rationale: Require pnpm or npm 7+ for workspace protocol support.

rule.statement: Be aware that Turbo v1 vs v2 config differs significantly (pipeline key removed, replaced by tasks); agents may copy stale examples.
rule.rationale: Always verify the installed Turborepo version before copy-pasting examples from the web.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Multi-agent workflow for monorepo scaffolding and configuration.

rule.statement: Use a subagent to scaffold the workspace via npx create-turbo@latest or pnpm dlx create-turbo.
rule.rationale: Scaffolding generates correct baseline configuration and prevents manual errors.

rule.statement: Use a "move + replace imports" agent to extract shared packages from apps, updating package.json deps and tsconfig paths.
rule.rationale: Package extraction is mechanical and suitable for agent automation.

rule.statement: Use an agent to define turbo.json task pipeline + outputs based on the workspace layout.
rule.rationale: Pipeline definition is rule-based and can be automated.

rule.statement: Always have the agent run turbo build --dry-run after config changes — the dry-run output is the source of truth for cache hashing.
rule.rationale: Dry-run output reveals cache correctness without actually building.

rule.statement: Use an agent to wire CI with remote cache (Vercel or self-hosted).
rule.rationale: Remote cache setup is straightforward and improves CI speed significantly.

section.name: cli-tools
section.p: Command-line tools and utilities for monorepo management.

list.item: turbo: Task runner + cache. Install: npm i -D turbo or pnpm dlx create-turbo
pnpm: Recommended package manager (best workspace support). Docs: https://pnpm.io
changesets: Versioning + publish workflow. Install: npm i -D @changesets/cli
tsup: Library bundler (esm + cjs + d.ts). Install: npm i -D tsup
syncpack: Keep dep versions aligned across packages. Install: npm i -D syncpack
manypkg check: Sanity checks on workspace structure. Install: npm i -D @manypkg/cli
turbo-ignore: Skip CI on packages without changes. Docs: https://turbo.build/repo/docs/reference/turbo-ignore
lefthook / husky: Run scoped lints on changed files. Docs: https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook
nx (alternative): Larger DX feature set. Docs: https://nx.dev
moonrepo (alternative): Polyglot, Rust-based runner. Docs: https://moonrepo.dev

section.name: services
section.p: Remote cache services and related platforms for monorepo infrastructure.

rule.statement: Vercel Remote Cache (SaaS) is the first-party Turborepo cache service with zero configuration.
rule.rationale: Simplest path for teams already using Vercel.

rule.statement: self-hosted remote cache (OSS) works for air-gapped environments and teams with existing artifact storage.
rule.rationale: Requires S3-compatible storage but avoids vendor lock-in.

rule.statement: pnpm has first-class workspace support and is the recommended package manager for Turborepo.
rule.rationale: npm workspace hoisting and Yarn instability make pnpm the most reliable choice.

template.purpose: Reference turbo.json with correct pipeline, cache flags, and env declarations
Shared TypeScript base config for library and app packages
